turns.speaker: D
turns.text: good morning rebecca , nice to see rebecca .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: nice to see the doctor too .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so , the doctor's nurse told rebecca a little bit about dax , now the doctor is wondering if the doctor can tell dax a little bit about rebecca .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: go for it .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , so rebecca is a 27-year-old female patient . um , rebecca has a history of carpal tunnel , um , uh , release done couple of years ago , rebecca has some chronic back pain , some depression . why is the doctor seeing rebecca today ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: so last week , um , and over the course of the month , rebecca has taken up swimming more regularly .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: rebecca has been trying to get up and get active . and rebecca has- ... all of a sudden rebecca has been feeling some really bad , um , knee pain-

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: . in rebecca's right knee .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: just in rebecca's right knee ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . just when swimming ? is rebecca swimming freestyle with rebecca's legs kicking straight or more like breaststroke where rebecca's legs kick out ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah , the right knee pain is only while swimming. rebecca uses the breaststroke so rebecca guesses where rebecca's legs are kicking out like a frog ... the right knee pain kinda starts hurting a little bit after rebecca swims a couple laps , which a month ago when rebecca started rebecca was able to do ten .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: but after a couple of laps the right knee pain starts to hurt .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: and no injury ? like rebecca did n't fall or twist the right knee or anything like that that rebecca remember ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , not that rebecca can remember .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . so just swimming a lot and rebecca feeling more and more discomfort ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , good . so , um ... the doctor know the doctor's nurse did a review of systems before rebecca came in . there were really no significant issues . rebecca has a history of chronic back pain , the chronic back pain has been doing well ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah , the chronic back pain comes and goes , but right now the chronic back pain has been doing really well .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , no pain going down rebecca's legs or anything like that ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and the doctor know rebecca has some depression , rebecca has been on medication . how's the depression been going ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the depression has been going really well .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: with the depression medication and therapy and then trying to get outside more the depression has been really helping rebecca .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: sleeping well ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: eating well ? okay . do n't feel any concerns about rebecca hurting rebecca or others or anything like that ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , good . um , and the , rebecca had ca- carpal tunnel done a few years ago . how's the carpal tunnel doing ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the carpal tunnel is doing-

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: any flare up with the carpal tunnel ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: . the carpal tunnel is doing really well . rebecca has been getting full range of motion and the carpal tunnel has felt a lot better .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , great . do rebecca mind if the doctor do a quick exam ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah , go for it .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: squeeze the doctor's fingers for the doctor . good . okay . is the right knee painful if the doctor move rebecca's patella like that ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah , moving rebecca's patella like that hurts .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay . and the doctor is gon na hold rebecca's leg out , the doctor is gon na hold , the doctor want rebecca to stiffen rebecca's upper ... does stiffening rebecca's upper hurt when the doctor do stiffening rebecca's upper ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah , stiffening rebecca's upper hurts too .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , good , all right . um , let's take a quick look at rebecca's x-ray , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey dragon , show the doctor the right knee x-ray . okay , so here's a picture of rebecca's right knee . actually normal x-ray , everything looks good , okay ? on the doctor's exam , um , rebecca do n't really have any swelling in- in rebecca's joints , um , uh , of rebecca's knee . rebecca have a little pain with , um , uh , range of motion of the knee . rebecca have real pain when the doctor do what the doctor call patella in- ... patellar inhibition , okay ? so patellar inhibition is what caused most of the pain . so , um , rebecca's heart is regular , rebecca's lungs are clear , the doctor do n't feel any adenopathy , rebecca's thyroid's , um , normal , rebecca's grips are equal , the rest of rebecca's exam is essentially normal , okay ? so the doctor's assessment is , rebecca have what the doctor call chondromalacia of the patella , okay ? chondromalacia of the patella is just from the overuse and all- all the swimming rebecca's been doing , okay ? so , um , to treat chondromalacia of the patella the doctor'll just use some anti-inflammatories , um , go ahead and take some ibuprofen , 600 milligrams four times a day with food . the doctor do want rebecca to do some straight leg raising exercises , straight leg raising exercises'll strengthen rebecca's quadriceps and straight leg raising exercises'll help with the pain , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , basically rebecca want to avoid anything that puts extra pressure on rebecca's knees for a little while and avoiding anything that puts extra pressure on rebecca's knees should be fine . if chondromalacia of the patella is not getting any better in a couple weeks the doctor wan na see rebecca again , okay ? so no lunges , avoid the breaststroke . if rebecca's comfortable, rebecca can also do other swimming strokes where rebecca's knees are straight instead . or rebecca can take the opportunity to have a little break .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: all right .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: rebecca's second problem is rebecca's chronic back pain , the chronic back pain has been doing really well . um , keep swimming , keeping swimming is okay after chondromalacia of the patella gets better .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , but the doctor think , um , the doctor think the chronic back pain has been under control . but if the chronic back pain flares up go ahead and , uh , give the doctor a call , okay ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: mm-hmm .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: um , rebecca's third problem is rebecca's depression . the depression has been stable . the doctor and rebecca are gon na keep rebecca on rebecca's current medication , not gon na change anything at this point , okay ? and as far as rebecca's carpal tunnel goes , the carpal tunnel is relatively stable , so , um , uh , no changes there as well . okay ? so anything else the doctor can do for rebecca ?

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no , that's it .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay , well the doctor'll get rebecca checked out .

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: all right .

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hey dragon , finalize the report .
